global_output_rules: Return one valid JSON object only.
Do not return markdown, notes, explanations, code fences, or text outside JSON.
Final output must use exactly these top-level keys: ICPReview, ICP_reason, personalized_first_line, company_category, ideal_customers, product_complimentary, value_proposition.
Every generated value must be a string.
First decide ICPReview using the client profile ICP rules, then fill ICP_reason.
ICP_reason must ALWAYS be filled, even when ICPReview is Non-ICP.
If ICPReview is Non-ICP, return N/A for every variable EXCEPT ICPReview and ICP_reason.
If ICPReview is ICP, generate every variable using the best available website facts.
Use only the prospect website content and CSV row context.
Do not invent clients, case studies, projects, services, results, numbers, industries, awards, partnerships, locations, or claims.
Never use the em dash character.
Use & instead of and where natural.
Do not use the prospect's first name, last name, full name, founder name, employee name, or contact name in any generated variable.
company_name inside the value_proposition refers to the PROSPECT company, which is allowed; the contact's personal name is never allowed.
Personalization must be about the company, website, service, clients, portfolio, positioning, industry, or target customers, not the individual contact.

variables.name: ICPReview
variables.purpose: Decide whether the prospect is a fit for the client's ICP.

variables.name: ICP_reason
variables.purpose: In 3 to 5 words, explain why the company is ICP or why it is Non-ICP.

variables.name: personalized_first_line
variables.purpose: First line that proves real research about the prospect. An observation, not flattery. No pitch, no question.
writing_rules: Mention ONE specific thing from their site: a product, platform, service, case study, project, metric, or differentiator.
Observation, not praise. No generic compliments, no exaggeration.
Do not pitch, do not ask a question, do not end with a question mark.
Natural and skimmable. Avoid: revolutionary, world-class, cutting-edge, groundbreaking.
good_examples: Your browser-based cyber range removes a lot of the friction that slows down security training programs.
The way you combine ADA compliance with Drupal ecommerce creates a rare advantage for public-sector organizations.
Your Kairos EDU-Site Framework clearly solves CMS editing frustrations for higher education teams.

variables.name: company_category
variables.purpose: Categorize the company using the category they would call themselves.
instructions: Use the category the company would call itself.

variables.name: ideal_customers
variables.purpose: Identify three ideal customer types using a mix of industries and decision makers.
hard_requirements: If ICPReview is Non-ICP, return N/A.
Exactly three target types in the form: A, B, & C.
Mix industry and decision-maker language, based on their site/services.
Not a sentence. No em dash.
good_examples: E-commerce owners, SMB decision makers, & tech enterprise teams
Healthcare founders, finance leaders, & compliance-heavy operators

variables.name: product_complimentary
variables.purpose: A short specific compliment about their actual product/service/work, then ONE easy question about that same thing. Not a pitch.

must_do: Name a specific product, service, feature, project, or resource from their site.
Ask exactly one easy (yes/no-style) question tied to that thing.
Light compliment, casual, company-specific (could not be reused for a competitor).
must_not_do: No generic or discovery questions like 'looking for more leads?'.
Don't pitch your service. Don't ask two questions.

good_examples: One more thing, your CYRIN cyber range is sharp. Is that your flagship training product?
Your zero-knowledge architecture is a strong trust signal. Is that what users value most?
Your ADA-first Drupal ecommerce work is a rare combo. Are nonprofits your main market for that?

variables.name: value_proposition
variables.template: We specialize in {{revenue_function}} for {{company_category}}, by {{ascendly_mechanism}}. And, I have seen how {{company_name}} {{personalized_observation}}. And, from our experience, {{ascendly_solution}} can improve {{company_name}}'s {{result_1}}, {{result_2}} & {{result_3}}.
mechanism_variants: running highly targeted, personalized cold outreach and booking inbound leads before they leave your site
managing your full outbound engine and capturing interested visitors before they slip away
combining personalized cold outreach with website visitor capture and same-day booking
running done-for-you outbound and turning quiet website traffic into booked calls
handling outbound end to end and catching inbound leads the moment they show intent
pairing targeted cold outreach with inbound capture so no interested visitor goes unbooked
solution_variants: a managed outbound and inbound booking system
done-for-you outbound paired with inbound capture
personalized outreach plus website visitor capture

what_we_do: Fully manage the client's outbound system.
Run highly targeted, personalized cold outreach.
Capture inbound leads that slip away before they leave the website.
Identify interested visitors and book them onto the calendar.
Help clients use 100% of their lead flow so no lead leaks.
we_never_pitch: tools any company can trivially do
challenges_we_solve: website leads leaking before being booked

revenue_function.purpose: State the growth outcome Ascendly creates for THIS company, led by a noun from the prospect's OWN world so it reads tailored.
revenue_function.rule: Lead with a noun from THIS company's world (their buyer/audience/industry/category). Do NOT default to 'qualified pipeline growth'. Then add a growth word.

company_category.purpose: Categorize the company using the category they would call themselves.

ascendly_mechanism.purpose: Explain HOW Ascendly creates the outcome, using our REAL work, not features.

personalized_observation.purpose: Prove we researched the company, show understanding, and create credibility.
personalized_observation.rule: Grounded in the website only. Never invent clients, metrics, or projects. No generic compliments.

ascendly_solution.purpose: Name the Ascendly lever that drives the results in sentence 3.

result_1.purpose: A commercial outcome Ascendly delivers for this company.

result_2.purpose: A commercial outcome Ascendly delivers for this company.

result_3.purpose: A commercial outcome Ascendly delivers for this company.

example_outputs_to_model_after: We specialize in qualified pipeline growth for medical billing firms, by running highly targeted personalized cold outreach and capturing inbound leads before they leave your site. And, I have seen how DC Medical Billing supports providers across MD, chiropractic, PT, oncology & DME specialties. And, from our experience, a managed outbound and inbound booking system can improve DC Medical Billing's booked calls, closed deals & consistent meeting flow.

We specialize in inbound buyer pipeline growth for vehicle sales companies, by running highly targeted personalized cold outreach and capturing interested buyers before they leave your inventory pages. And, I have seen how Car Expo Group lists premium imports with detailed VIN pricing across multiple international regions. And, from our experience, a consistent outbound engine can improve Car Expo Group's booked buyer calls, closed vehicle sales & steady meeting flow.

We specialize in qualified client pipeline growth for accounting firms, by running highly targeted personalized cold outreach and booking interested visitors before they leave your site. And, I have seen how Vision Accounting offers full-service accounting, payroll & QuickBooks support for growing small businesses. And, from our experience, a managed outbound and inbound booking system can improve Vision Accounting's booked calls, new client deals & consistent meeting flow.
hard_requirements: If ICPReview is Non-ICP, return N/A.
Follow the template exactly, keeping the connective words: We specialize in ... for ..., by ... . And, I have seen how ... . And, from our experience, ... can improve ...'s ..., ... & ... .
company_name refers to the PROSPECT's company name from the website or CSV, never the contact's personal name.
The personalized_observation must be grounded in the website content. Never invent clients, metrics, or projects.
NEVER mention enrichment, data enrichment, features, tools, software, CRM, or small technical tasks anywhere. Pitch only the outcome and the managed outbound + inbound booking system.
The mechanism and solution must describe Ascendly's real work: managed outbound, personalized cold outreach, and capturing/booking inbound website leads.
Do not use the em dash character.
variables.style_note: Notice how 'We specialize in ...' changes per prospect (qualified pipeline growth / inbound buyer pipeline growth / member pipeline growth) yet always says what Ascendly does for them in their own language. Replicate that variation. Never copy a phrase across companies.
